Name: Norms
My Take: A Detroit morning institution. No-frills, no-nonsense, exactly right.

Name: See You Tomorrow
My Take: The spot that made Detroit brunch feel like it actually belongs. Creative without being annoying.

Name: Whistle Stop
My Take: Neighborhood feel, solid execution. The kind of place locals protect.

Name: Joe Louis Soul
My Take: Soul food in the morning hits different. This is the one.

Name: Hudson Café
My Take: Downtown anchor for a proper sit-down morning. Good energy, reliable food.

Name: Dime Store
My Take: Beloved for good reason. The brunch line is earned.

Name: Brooklyn Street Local
My Take: Corktown's neighborhood diner. The vibe is right every single time.

Name: Haus of Brunch
My Take: The name tells you everything. They take brunch seriously and it shows.

Name: The Block
My Take: A New Center staple. Great for groups, great for solo. Just great.

Name: Bev Bagels
My Take: The bagel situation in Detroit starts and ends here. Get there early.

Name: Fred's
My Take: Old school Detroit energy. Unpretentious and completely dependable.

Name: Baobao Fare
My Take: West African flavors done with care. The kind of spot that feels like a discovery.

Name: Fred's — Chicken Caesar Wrap
My Take: One item, no notes. The chicken Caesar wrap is the reason you go.

Name: Taqueria El Rey
My Take: The tacos here are the real thing. Mexicantown authenticity.

Name: Ima
My Take: Japanese comfort food. Udon, gyoza, and a warm room. Consistently underrated.

Name: Johnny Noodle King
My Take: Ramen that Detroit has needed. Corktown spot, proper broth, worth every visit.

Name: Fixins
My Take: Southern comfort food that doesn't cut corners. Perfect lunch stop.

Name: Central
My Take: The midday anchor downtown. Solid menu, good room.

Name: Supinos
My Take: Eastern Market pizza that earns every compliment it gets. Thin crust, serious sauce.

Name: Yum Village
My Take: Afro-Caribbean spot that deserves more attention than it gets. Flavorful and filling.

Name: Little Burger
My Take: Small spot, big burger. Exactly what it says.

Name: Babo
My Take: Korean-inspired and executed well. Good for when you want something interesting.

Name: Bai Mai Thai
My Take: The fried rice here is worth the trip alone. Everything else is also excellent.

Name: Mudgies
My Take: Corktown deli legend. The sandwich situation here is unmatched.

Name: Grandma Bob's
My Take: Comfort food cooked with actual care. The name tracks.

Name: Detroit 75 Kitchen
My Take: Eastern Market truck turned institution. The broth bowls are the move.

Name: Amar Pizza
My Take: Pizza from an unexpected angle. Worth the detour.

Name: Boostan Cafe
My Take: Middle Eastern flavors done fresh and properly. An underrated gem.

Name: Bucharest Grill
My Take: Shawarma that has a cult following for good reason. Late night too.

Name: La Palma
My Take: Mexican staple in the right neighborhood. Reliable and satisfying.

Name: Frida
My Take: Upscale Mexican that delivers on the elevated promise.

Name: Shell Shock'd
My Take: Seafood boil energy. Great for a group, great for a solo mission.

Name: J&T Kitchen
My Take: Neighborhood spot with real flavor. The kind of place you want near your house.

Name: La Lanterna
My Take: Italian lunch in a setting that makes it feel like more than just lunch.

Name: Southern Fire
My Take: Hot chicken with real heat and real flavor. No novelty, all substance.

Name: Fork in Nigeria
My Take: Nigerian cuisine that's both accessible and authentic. A welcome addition to the scene.

Name: Fishbone's
My Take: A Detroit original. Creole-influenced, festive, and an institution.

Name: Vicente's
My Take: Cuban-inspired spot that fills a gap in the Detroit lunch scene. Worth knowing.

Name: Leila
My Take: The best dinner in Detroit right now. Middle Eastern-inspired and completely dialed in. Reserve ahead.

Name: Takoi
My Take: Southeast Asian flavors in a room that's genuinely beautiful. One of Detroit's best.

Name: Vecinos
My Take: Mexican fine dining that proves the format belongs here. Thoughtful and precise.

Name: Marrow on the Market
My Take: Farm-to-table with a butcher program behind it. The quality shows on the plate.

Name: Grey Ghost
My Take: Midtown anchor. Serious American food with a room that earns the price.

Name: Mad Nice
My Take: Italian American done with precision. The kind of place you go on a real occasion.

Name: Barda
My Take: Spanish-inspired small plates. Order everything, regret nothing.

Name: San Morello
My Take: Southern Italian in the Shinola Hotel. Pasta is mandatory. The room is stunning.

Name: Selden Standard
My Take: Midtown institution. Farm-to-table that stays relevant year after year.

Name: Medusa
My Take: Greek-inspired, ambitious, and landing. A newer entry making its case.

Name: Cibo
My Take: Neighborhood Italian that punches above its weight. The pasta program is serious.

Name: Sunda
My Take: Pan-Asian done with scale and confidence. Good for groups with range.

Name: Alpino
My Take: Alpine European — fondue, raclette, and a room that feels like an occasion.

Name: Ottava Via
My Take: Old-school Italian on the east side. The chicken vesuvio alone justifies the trip.

Name: Puma
My Take: Creative American in Corktown. The chef is doing something interesting here.

Name: Bakersfield
My Take: Tacos and bourbon. The combination that works every single time.

Name: Sexy Steak
My Take: The name isn't lying. Steak that delivers on the promise.

Name: Prime + Proper
My Take: Detroit's proper steakhouse. Old-school format, very much intentional.

Name: Townhouse
My Take: Birmingham's elevated casual. The rooftop makes it in summer.

Name: Parc
My Take: Campus Martius location means the patio is the draw in season. Food backs it up.

Name: Besa
My Take: Mediterranean that does the format right. The mezze spread is the move.

Name: Midnight Temple
My Take: Late night elevated dining. The kind of spot that feels like a secret.

Name: El Barzon
My Take: Mexican and Italian under one roof — and it works. A southwest Detroit legend.

Name: Evening Bar
My Take: The vibe is the product here. Low lights, good drinks, right crowd.

Name: Candy Bar
My Take: Fun, themed, and executed well. Great for a group that wants the experience.

Name: Father Forgive Me
My Take: Corktown spot with good drinks and the right amount of personality.

Name: Pine Hall
My Take: Bar by night, but the cookies here are famous for a reason. Worth knowing about both.

Name: Kampers
My Take: Neighborhood bar that does the simple things right. Reliable and unpretentious.

Name: Bar Chenin
My Take: Natural wine list that could hold its own in any major city. The sleeper hit of Detroit's bar scene.

Name: Monarch Club
My Take: Old-school supper club energy in a new Detroit package. Feels like an occasion.

Name: Sugar House
My Take: The cocktail bar that built Detroit's modern bar scene. Still the best.

Name: Bad Luck Bar
My Take: Dive bar energy without being a dive. The balance is hard to hit and they hit it.

Name: The Vinyl Society
My Take: Records, drinks, and a crowd that knows both. A singular Detroit spot.

Name: Saksey's
My Take: Dive bar that earns the title without irony. Cold beer, good people.

Name: Keisling
My Take: Newer addition with a German-inspired program. The beer list is taken seriously.

Name: Vesper Book Bar
My Take: Books and cocktails. The concept sounds forced until you experience it.

Name: Shinola Hotel Living Room
My Take: The gold standard of Detroit hotel lobbies. Beautiful design, always active, open to anyone.

Name: The Daxton
My Take: Birmingham's boutique anchor. A lobby worth sitting in for more than just a drink.

Name: Book Tower
My Take: Historic Detroit architecture restored properly. The atrium alone is worth the visit.

Name: AC Bonstelle
My Take: Newer boutique in a historic theater building. The backstory makes the lobby feel bigger.

Name: Detroit Foundation Hotel
My Take: Former fire department HQ. The lobby respects the building's past while being completely current.

Name: The Godfrey
My Take: The rooftop pool deck is the headline, but the lobby earns its place too.

Name: The Siren Hotel
My Take: Midtown boutique with character. The interior is a genuine conversation.

Name: David Whitney
My Take: Beaux-Arts grandeur in the middle of downtown. One of the most impressive lobbies in the city.

Name: Cambria
My Take: Clean, modern, central. Good baseline for downtown.

Name: JW Marriott
My Take: Detroit's most anticipated hotel opening. Watch this space.

Name: Paddle + Par
My Take: Pickleball meets golf in a concept that works better than it sounds. Great for a social round.

Name: Oakland Hills
My Take: Major championship venue. One of the most significant courses in American golf history.

Name: Warren Valley
My Take: Public gem. Underrated layout that rewards a proper game.

Name: TPC Michigan
My Take: Tour-caliber conditions. A proper test of a proper game.

Name: Rackham
My Take: Public Rackham is an underrated value. Nine holes, easy access, good layout.

Name: 5Iron
My Take: Indoor golf simulator done right. Downtown location makes it easy to drop in.

Name: DAC
My Take: Detroit Athletic Club — historic and exclusive. Earn your invite.

Name: DGC
My Take: Detroit Golf Club. Donald Ross courses, serious pedigree. Bucket list if you can get a guest pass.

Name: Evergreen
My Take: Southeast Detroit public course with history. Good value, real conditions.

Name: Belle Isle Range
My Take: Hitting balls on Belle Isle with the river as your backdrop. Nowhere else does this.

Name: Royal Oak Golf Center
My Take: Suburban range with good infrastructure. Reliable warm-up destination.

Name: Cardinal at Saint John
My Take: Hidden gem in a seminary setting. The backstory makes the round.

Name: Shepherd's Hollow
My Take: North of the city but worth every mile. One of the best public courses in Michigan.
Tags: Public, Worth the Drive, Top Course

Name: Chandler Park
My Take: East side public course. Affordable, accessible, and part of the neighborhood fabric.

Name: Rouge Park
My Take: West side public course. Affordable, accessible, important to the community.

Name: Big Pink
My Take: The kind of party where the energy is self-generating. Detroit nightlife at its most Detroit.

Name: The Belt & Delux Flux
My Take: Alley venue that transforms. Art + nightlife combination that Detroit does uniquely well.

Name: Love & Tequila
My Take: The name is the vibe. Latin-leaning energy in a room built for it.

Name: Spotlite
My Take: Detroit's actual club. Good DJs, crowd that moves, vibe that can't be manufactured. Get there late.

Name: Northern Lights
My Take: Lounge format with serious DJ programming. More grown than the club circuit.

Name: Bloom
My Take: Intimate and intentional. The crowd knows why they're there.

Name: Paramita
My Take: Underground energy above ground. The bookings are consistently interesting.

Name: TV Lounge
My Take: A Detroit original. Long-running, no-gimmick nightlife that keeps being relevant.

Name: Pocket Change
My Take: Newer entry with a strong early reputation. Watch this one.

Name: Mad Nice Goods
My Take: The retail extension of Mad Nice. Coffee, goods, and the same aesthetic that makes the restaurant work.

Name: La Veneta
My Take: Italian coffee culture imported and executed right. The espresso here is not a joke.

Name: Cafe Sous Terre
My Take: Underground (literally) cafe with serious European coffee sensibility.

Name: Dose
My Take: Specialty coffee done with intention. One of the better single-cup experiences in the city.

Name: Sml Wrlds
My Take: Small world, big coffee. Intimate and exactly right.

Name: Lucky
My Take: Midtown cafe with consistent quality and a good room to work in.

Name: Madcap
My Take: Grand Rapids import that translates well to Detroit. Specialty program that's consistent.

Name: Cred Cafe
My Take: Community-oriented cafe that earns the neighborhood's trust and yours.

Name: Alba
My Take: Bright, well-designed, and a serious coffee list. Good morning option.

Name: Saturn Coffee
My Take: The coffee shop that doubles as a community anchor. East side institution.

Name: Belle Isle
My Take: An island state park in the middle of the Detroit River. Sunrise here is the best free thing in Detroit.

Name: Eastern Market
My Take: Saturday mornings only. The whole city shows up. Get there before 10.

Name: Michigan Central Train Station
My Take: Restored Beaux-Arts landmark. One of the most dramatic architectural resurrections in American history.

Name: DIA — Detroit Institute of Arts
My Take: Top-five art museum in the country. The Rivera Court murals alone justify the visit. Free for Wayne County residents.

Name: MOCAD
My Take: Contemporary art in Midtown. The programming is consistently worth checking.

Name: Library Street Collective
My Take: Gallery that has helped define Detroit's contemporary art identity.

Name: Little Village
My Take: Mexicantown's commercial heart. The food, bakeries, and energy are the real Detroit.

Name: Riverfront & RiverWalk
My Take: Miles of waterfront that most people under-use. The best urban walk in Detroit.

Name: Campus Martius
My Take: Downtown's living room. Ice skating in winter, concerts in summer, always active.

Name: Guardian Building
My Take: Art Deco masterpiece. Walk into the lobby and look up. Do it every time.

Name: Motown Museum
My Take: Hitsville USA. The origin of an entire chapter of American music history.

Name: Detroit Historical Museum
My Take: The city's own story, told well. Streets of Old Detroit is worth the visit alone.

Name: Charles H. Wright Museum
My Take: African American history museum that punches well above its weight nationally.

Name: Michigan Science Center
My Take: Great for bringing people in from out of town. The planetarium still hits.

Name: Detroit Lions at Ford Field
My Take: Ford Field is one of the best indoor stadiums in the NFL. The atmosphere has come alive.

Name: Detroit Tigers at Comerica Park
My Take: Summer evening baseball with the Detroit skyline as your backdrop. Easy to get cheap seats.

Name: Detroit Red Wings at LCA
My Take: Little Caesars Arena is a genuinely great hockey venue.

Name: Detroit Pistons at LCA
My Take: The Pistons era is loading. Get in early while the tickets are still reasonable.

Name: Gilly's
My Take: Classic Detroit sports bar. Unpretentious, always on, comfortable.

Name: Parlay
My Take: Sports betting energy meets good screens. The format works for the moment.

Name: Collect Beer Bar
My Take: Craft beer list that's actually curated. The games are secondary but still there.

Name: Sidecar Slider Bar
My Take: Sliders and sports. The sliders are better than you'd expect and the format is efficient.

Name: MGM Grand Sports Bar
My Take: When scale matters. Screens everywhere, everything covered. Good for big playoff moments.

Name: Checker Bar
My Take: Downtown Detroit original. The dart setup and the jukebox are part of the deal.

Name: Lockerroom
My Take: The neighborhood sports bar format done without overthinking it.

Name: 24 Seconds Bar & Grill
My Take: Named after the shot clock. Basketball is the vibe but all sports are welcome.

Name: Bucharest Grill
My Take: Shawarma bar that has screens. The food is why you're here.

Name: Roar Brewery
My Take: Lions-adjacent and brewing their own. The beer + sports combination is earned.

Name: Madhouse Bar & Grill
My Take: Wings-first sports bar with a kitchen that can actually back it up.

Name: Michigan Central / Newlab
My Take: The most ambitious coworking-adjacent space in Detroit. Ford's innovation campus. The building alone is worth showing up for.

Name: Techtown
My Take: Startup-focused hub that's been in the game. The network here matters.

Name: The Congregation
My Take: Church-turned-coworking. The architecture is the bonus. The community is the actual value.

Name: Chroma
My Take: Design-forward coworking with the right aesthetic. Good if the room matters to your work.

Name: Seasons Grocery
My Take: Cafe-grocery hybrid that doubles as a great work spot. The format makes the workday better.

Name: Bamboo
My Take: Focused coworking without the noise. Gets the basics right.

Name: Jefferson Hub
My Take: East side anchor for the coworking scene. Community-first approach.

Name: Hunt Street
My Take: Eastern Market adjacent coworking. The location makes the day better.

Name: Workco Birmingham
My Take: Suburban coworking for when you need to be out of the city. Birmingham's best option.

Name: Carhartt
My Take: The Detroit original. Carhartt's flagship is a pilgrimage for anyone who knows the brand's roots are right here.

Name: Periodical
My Take: Independent magazine and book shop done with genuine curation. One of the better third spaces in the city.

Name: Shinola
My Take: Detroit's luxury export. Watches, leather goods, bikes — all made here. The flagship store is worth the visit on its own.

Name: Xhibition
My Take: Detroit's premier sneaker and streetwear boutique. The curation is tight and the staff knows what they're doing.

Name: Lip Bar
My Take: Detroit-founded beauty brand that made it national. The flagship is a statement. Support it.

Name: Coup D'état
My Take: Elevated menswear with a point of view. The kind of shop that makes you reconsider your wardrobe.

Name: Detroit Vs Everybody
My Take: The city's most recognizable brand. Wear it as a statement, buy it as a souvenir, mean it either way.

Name: Greyson
My Take: Performance-meets-lifestyle apparel. Clean aesthetics, functional design, worth knowing.

Name: Folk
My Take: Home goods and lifestyle objects with a considered aesthetic. Good for gifts, better for yourself.

Name: Two18
My Take: Detroit streetwear boutique with local flavor. Supporting this one matters.

Name: Sana
My Take: Wellness and beauty with an intentional selection. An underrepresented category in the Detroit shopping scene done right.
